root_text.sn35.149:1.3: katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, nibbānasappāyā paṭipadā?
root_text.sn35.149:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cakkhuṁ anattāti passati, rūpā anattāti passati, cakkhuviññāṇaṁ anattāti passati, cakkhusamphasso anattāti passati, yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi anattāti passati …pe…
root_text.sn35.149:1.5: mano anattāti passati, dhammā anattāti passati, manoviññāṇaṁ anattāti passati, manosamphasso anattāti passati, yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi anattāti passati.
root_text.sn35.149:1.6: Ayaṁ kho sā, bhikkhave, nibbānasappāyā paṭipadā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.149:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you a practice that’s conducive to extinguishment.
translation_text.sn35.149:1.3: And what is that practice that’s conducive to extinguishment?
translation_text.sn35.149:1.4: It’s when a mendicant sees that the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact are not-self. And they see that the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact is also not-self.
translation_text.sn35.149:1.5: They see that the ear … nose … tongue … body … mind, ideas, mind-consciousness, and mind contact are not-self. And they see that the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact is also not-self.
translation_text.sn35.149:1.6: This is that practice that’s conducive to extinguishment.”
